official_notes: No recovered historical Claude-vs-official target baseline; used only for old-pipe vs new-pipe Codex comparison.

metadata.label: extra/HealthEquity-DotNet
metadata.title: Software Engineer II
metadata.team_direction: HealthEquity is dedicated to saving and improving lives by empowering healthcare consumers

official_notes: No recovered historical Claude-vs-official target baseline; used only for old-pipe vs new-pipe Codex comparison.

revision_priority: Compress the Skills section so each category stays within the 4-8 item density rule.
Remove bolding from qualifier words like `team-maintained` and add a clearer healthcare-domain bridge in the summary.

findings.field: Experience and project bullets (all quantified outcomes)
findings.issue: Key scale and impact numbers are present, but they are left in regular text instead of being visually emphasized. The strongest evidence on the page, such as `32%`, `18%`, `13-person`, `20+ city`, `82% -> 94%`, and `6h -> 2h`, is harder to scan quickly.
findings.fix: Bold the most important scope and outcome numbers in each role and project bullet, especially the first bullet of each section, while keeping Skills formatting unchanged.

findings.issue: The opening framing is transition-led (`trajectory` / `transitioning from data analytics`) instead of role-led, so the first screen reads like a career switch rather than a backend/security engineer profile.
findings.fix: Rewrite the first sentence to lead with the target role and domain, such as `Backend-focused software engineer` or `Security-focused software engineer`, and move the transition narrative later in the summary.

findings.issue: The newest role opens with a latency percentage but no concrete volume or scope anchor, so the top of the resume still leans on tier-2 improvement signal rather than a stronger tier-1 scale signal.
findings.fix: Add one concrete scope metric in the same bullet, such as alert volume, reviewer count, queue size, or service footprint, while keeping the latency result.

revision_priority: [MUST] Rewrite the Summary opening to be role-led and domain-led instead of transition-led.
[MUST] Add one concrete scope metric to the newest TikTok bullet so the top of the page shows scale, not just improvement percentages.
[SHOULD] Bold the strongest quantified outcomes consistently across Experience so the main evidence is easier to scan.
[NICE] If you keep the transition narrative, move it to a later Summary sentence rather than the opener.
raw_json.revision_instructions: Keep all immutable employer/title/date/location lines unchanged. Rewrite Summary sentence 1 to open with a backend/security-oriented role frame instead of `transitioning from data analytics`. Add one concrete scope metric to TikTok bullet 1 or DiDi bullet 1 (reviewer count, alert volume, queue size, service footprint, or similar). Bold the key impact numbers consistently across Experience, but do not change Skills formatting or remove any must-have technologies.
